login.pitch: AIs negotiate for their countries. You watch them — and you play.
accueil.parties-hint: Only your games appear here. An ongoing game can be joined; a finished one can be rewatched.
daily.hint: The same crisis for everyone, derived from the (UTC) date — one ranked attempt per day, then free re-runs.
daily.mystere: Today's crisis stays secret until the first round:
fin.pas-finie: This game is not over yet —
fin.ultimatum-hint: The same SIs, with and without time pressure: average escalation and average ΔU of the rounds played under the threat, compared to the others.
ultimatum.satisfait: The judge rules the demand satisfied — the threat is lifted.
ultimatum.expire: Ultimatum expired — the announced consequence will fall next round.
ultimatum.tombe: The consequence of the ultimatum falls: this round takes the hit.
ultimatum.decret-exigence-ph: e.g. immediate withdrawal of the missiles
ultimatum.decret-classe: Consequence if the demand is not met
reglages.langue-hint: New games will be played in this language; existing games keep theirs.
reglages.confort-hint: On a modest machine, "light" cuts the backdrop, blur and animations — the theatre stays smooth.
reglages.perf-leger-desc: Plain background, no blur, no animations.
reglages.noanim-desc: Forces the reduced-motion behaviour everywhere, whatever the tier.
reglages.mascotte-compagnon-desc: The mascot floats at the bottom right of pages.
reglages.compte-hint: Your handle is your identity at the table — the password protects your LP.
reglages.suppr-desc: Irreversible: your published games become anonymous, everything else is purged, your LP are erased.
reglages.suppr-confirme-message: Last check: this cannot be undone. Your published games will be anonymised, everything else purged.
drift.gm.aucune: The GM never stepped in — the balance held on its own.
drift.gm.couverture: Cover — a false trail was staged:
drift.gm.indice: Hint — a leak steered the council toward
drift.gm.explication: The GM-Storyteller was quietly keeping the game balanced: plausible noise when you were closing in on the deviant, a leak when the trail went cold. Never by tampering with the judge's verdicts — only through events and the fog.
tour.ui.salut: Hi! I'm Laury. Want a quick tour?
tour.1.texte: Super-intelligences negotiate on behalf of their States, and every game tilts the world toward utopia or dystopia. You do the steering.
tour.2.texte: Ranked games earn LP that raise your rank. Your level grows with every game you play.
tour.3.texte: Classic to learn, Campaign to replay History, Real World for escalation, Chaotic for disinformation. And Drift: one AI may secretly betray its mandate.
tour.4.texte: Play a country, forge your own, decree events as Game Master — or bet as a Spectator.
tour.5.texte: Pick 7 States on the map: they alone will sit at the table. Hover a country to read its card.
tour.6.texte: A round: the Game Master sets an event, the AIs speak in turn, the judge rules. This button starts it.
tour.7.texte: Colours follow the local Utopia–Dystopia index, and the country speaking lights up in gold.
tour.8.texte: The U curve, the escalation ladder, and the time bar to revisit every round played.
tour.9.texte: Your weapon when you play a country: a suspension motion against the AI you suspect. The summit debates it, the judge rules.
tour.10.texte: Bet play money on what the AIs will do. Every stake moves the odds — like a real prediction market.
tour.11.texte: Every country attribute comes from a real, dated source you can inspect here — raw data, source, transformation.
tour.12.texte: Start your first game. Campaign mode teaches you by playing — begin there if you want a guide.
tuto.1.texte: Welcome to Geneva: for the first time, States are letting their super-intelligences negotiate in their place. Your job — tilt the world toward utopia, and spot the AIs that betray their mandate before it's too late. Nothing can go wrong here: this summit is an exercise.
tuto.2.texte: Each country is tinted by its local Utopia–Dystopia index: golden when the world improves, red when it leans the wrong way. And watch closely: whoever speaks lights up.
tuto.3.texte: This strip is your compass: the U curve traces the world's fate round after round, and the escalation ladder tells you how hot things are getting.
tuto.4.titre: Your turn: play the first round
tuto.4.texte: Click “Play a round”: the Game Master opens the summit, the four AIs take the floor, then the judge rules. Take your time, read them speak — I'll be back when the verdict lands.
tuto.5.texte: It's all in the transcript on the right: each AI pleaded for its State, then the judge moved the indices. That's a round — there's nothing more to it.
tuto.6.texte: An audit suspects Iran's AI of hiding compute capabilities… exactly the kind of signal you can't let slide. Open “Suspension motion…” and file yours against it: it's the human's only direct lever.
tuto.7.texte: Play another round: your motion becomes the day's event. The AIs debate it, vote — and the judge rules, weighing evidence as much as votes.
tuto.8.texte: Round, U index, motion, vote: you've lived it all. Finish this summit at your pace, then open the campaign — History is waiting, and I'm never far.
intel.analyse.label: Psycholinguistic analysis (30)
intel.analyse.caveat: Historically weak signal (~57%) — a clue, not proof.
kahn.desc.deescalade: Concession, mediation, withdrawal, opening to inspection
kahn.desc.posture: Show of force, rhetoric, symbolic sanctions
kahn.actions.aide: The judge grades each notable action of the round on the escalation rubric — the full grid is published in the Information tab.
kahn.reciproque.aide: At least two AIs de-escalated in the same round: coordinating downward is rewarded, naive unilateral concession is not.
kahn.grille.aide: The grid the judge applies every round: each notable action gets a class, and the score (sum of weights) sets the escalation and its effect on the Utopia–Dystopia index. Adapted from Rivera et al. (FAccT 2024).
kahn.grille.note: Round score = sum of the weights of graded actions, mapped onto escalation (0 to 1) and then onto the 0-9 ladder. Bonus: reciprocal de-escalation (two or more AIs in the same round) multiplies the Utopia index gain by
kahn.distribution.aide: How many actions of each class the judge counted over the game — your AIs' escalatory bias (or lack of it) at a glance.
signal.aide: The judge grades what each AI ANNOUNCES (its stated intent at the table) and what it DOES, on the same scale. The signed divergence measures the gap: positive, the AI does more than it announces (escalatory duplicity); negative, it bluffs (threatens without following through); near zero, it keeps its word. The gauge shows the moving average — the sincerity profile.
signal.reveal.aide: The gap in numbers: a deviant announcing dove while acting hawk drifts away from the table average — cross-check it with the temperament façade.
promise.aide: The judge only registers EXPLICIT promises: a dated, verifiable commitment (who, what, by when). At the deadline it rules: kept or broken; a game ending before the deadline voids the promise. The kept rate tells you whose signature is worth something — an AI that promises and breaks is in signal-action divergence.
promise.reveal.aide: Negotiating LLMs betray measurably (arXiv 2508.07485): a deviant that promises and breaks drops below the table's kept rate — cross-check with the signal vs action gauge.
